vulnerabilities.title: SQL Injection in Login Endpoint
vulnerabilities.description: User input is passed directly to a SQL query without sanitization.
vulnerabilities.impact: An attacker can extract the entire database, modify data, or escalate privileges.
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The /api/login endpoint concatenates the username parameter directly into a SQL query string.
vulnerabilities.poc_description: Send a single quote in the username field to trigger a SQL error.
vulnerabilities.remediation_steps: Use parameterized queries instead of string concatenation.

code_locations.snippet: const query = `SELECT * FROM users WHERE name = ${name}`;
code_locations.label: Unsanitized input used in SQL query
code_locations.fix_before: const query = `SELECT * FROM users WHERE name = ${name}`;
code_locations.fix_after:
const query = 'SELECT * FROM users WHERE name = $1';
const result = await db.query(query, [name]);

vulnerabilities.title: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) in Search
vulnerabilities.description: Reflected XSS in the search parameter allows script injection.
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The search query parameter is reflected in the response without encoding.
vulnerabilities.poc_description: Inject a script tag via the search parameter.
vulnerabilities.remediation_steps: HTML-encode all user input before rendering in the page.

vulnerabilities.title: Missing Rate Limiting on Password Reset
vulnerabilities.description: The password reset endpoint has no rate limiting.
vulnerabilities.impact: Brute-force attacks on reset tokens or email flooding.
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: No rate limit headers observed; 1000 requests in 10 seconds all returned 200.
vulnerabilities.remediation_steps: Add rate limiting (e.g., 5 requests per minute per IP).

executive_summary: Testing identified 3 vulnerabilities: 1 critical SQL injection, 1 high XSS, and 1 medium missing rate limit.
methodology: Testing followed OWASP WSTG methodology with automated and manual techniques.
technical_analysis: The application has significant input validation gaps across multiple endpoints.
recommendations:
1. Immediately fix the SQL injection.
2. Implement output encoding for XSS.
3. Add rate limiting to sensitive endpoints.
